name: Spinach-Stuffed Chicken Breasts with Medeira Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1-pound whole chicken breasts -- boned but not skinned
1/4 cup firmly packed cooked, squeezed, and chopped spinach -- (about 1/2 pound fresh spinach)
1 teaspoon arrowroot dissolved in 1 tablespoon water
recipeInstructions:
For the Stuffed Chicken:
1. Arrange the chicken breasts skin side down on a cutting board, making sure the skin is evenly stretched over the breasts.
2. Remove the fillet strip from each breast and discard the white tendon.
3. In a food processor, grind the fillets.
4. Add the ice and blend until absorbed.
5. With the motor running, add the cream.
6. Add the spinach, salt, fennel seeds, lemon rind, nutmeg, and pepper; blend well.
7. Turn the breasts skin side up and beginning at the pointed end pull the skin back carefully, leaving thin transparent membranes attached along the opposite end.
8. Spread 3 tablespoons of the spinach filling evenly over each breast, smoothing it.
9. Stretch the skin over the filling for another use such as miniature quenelles made by poaching small balls of it in clear soup.
10. Chill the chicken, wrapped tightly in plastic wrap for one hour.
For the Sauce:
1. Pour off the fat from the skillet.
2. Add Madeira and boil until reduced to about 2 tablespoons, scraping up brown bits.
3. Add broth and boil the mixture, stirring for 1 minute.
4. Stir arrowroot mixture, add it to the skillet, whisking, for 1 minute.
5. Holding a knife at a 45-degree angle, slice the chicken crosswise.
6. Arrange slices overlapping slightly on heated plates.
7. Spoon some remaining sauce separately.
